segments.text:
BIOMEMBRANES
Separate inside of cell from external environment
Also surround individual organelles

MEMBRANE LIPIDS
Amphipathic: contain both hydrophilic and hydrophobic regions
Phosphoglycerides
Sphingolipids
Sterols
Glycolipids

GLYCOLIPIDS
Lipid molecules with sugar(s) attached

LIPID ORGANIZATION
Bilayer
Polar heads on outside
Hydrophobic tails on inside

ARTIFICIAL MEMBRANES
Useful for membrane studies
LIPOSOME
BLACK MEMBRANE

MEMBRANE FLUIDITY
Necessary for function
Different types of movement

MEMBRANE PHASE TRANSITION
Membrane changes between gel-like and fluid-like state
Freezing point: temperature at which phase transition occurs

WHAT AFFECTS FREEZING POINT?
Unsaturated or short fatty acid tails
decrease freezing point
Saturated or long tails
increase freezing point

FRAP ASSAY
FRAP = fluorescence recovery after photobleaching
FRAP movie

LIPID RAFTS
Rich in sphingolipids and cholesterol
Don’t mix easily with other regions
May help organize membrane proteins

MEMBRANE ASYMMETRY
Essential for function
Exoplasmic monolayer: PC, SM, glycolipids
Cytosolic monolayer: PS, PE

ASYMMETRY EXAMPLE
Phosphatidylinositol (4,5) bisphosphate (PIP2)
Minor phospholipid
Found only in cytosolic monolayer (WHY?)

PIP2 FUNCTIONS IN CYTOSOLIC SIGNALING
Would be useless in outer monolayer!
